title: The Bus Is Late
summary: A man waits for a slow bus and finds something small but good.
body:
Tomás waits for the bus in Lima. The bus is late. He looks at his phone. There is no new message. He puts the phone away. He is bored.

Then Tomás looks at the street. He sees a small dog. The dog sleeps in the sun. A woman sells bread near the corner. The bread smells warm and good. An old man feeds the birds. The birds are not afraid of him.

Tomás watches the man and the birds. He never sees this before. He is always busy. He is always on his phone. Today the bus is late, so he has time. Time to look. Time to see his own street.

The bus comes after fifteen minutes. Tomás gets on the bus. He sits by the window. He thinks about the dog, the bread, and the old man. The late bus gives him a small gift. The gift is time. He smiles a little.

Now Tomás has a strange idea. Maybe a late bus is not always a bad thing. When he waits, he sees more. When he is fast, he sees nothing. He does not want the bus to be late every day. But today, the slow time is not so bad.

grammarNotes.title: Present simple for habits
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để nói về thói quen hằng ngày. Động từ thêm -s với he/she/it.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The story uses present simple for habits: "He is always busy" and "He is always on his phone."

grammarNotes.title: When for time
grammarNotes.bodyVi: "When" nối hai mệnh đề để nói khi nào điều gì xảy ra.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Look at "When he waits, he sees more" and "When he is fast, he sees nothing."

grammarNotes.title: so to show result
grammarNotes.bodyVi: "so" cho biết kết quả của một việc.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: In "the bus is late, so he has time," the word "so" shows the result.

translationVi:
Tomás đợi xe buýt ở Lima. Xe buýt đến muộn. Anh nhìn vào điện thoại. Không có tin nhắn mới nào. Anh cất điện thoại đi. Anh thấy chán.

Rồi Tomás nhìn ra đường phố. Anh thấy một con chó nhỏ. Con chó ngủ dưới nắng. Một người phụ nữ bán bánh mì gần góc đường. Bánh mì có mùi ấm và thơm. Một ông già cho chim ăn. Lũ chim không sợ ông.

Tomás nhìn ông già và lũ chim. Anh chưa từng thấy điều này trước đây. Anh lúc nào cũng bận. Anh lúc nào cũng dán mắt vào điện thoại. Hôm nay xe buýt đến muộn, nên anh có thời gian. Thời gian để nhìn. Thời gian để thấy con phố của chính mình.

Xe buýt đến sau mười lăm phút. Tomás lên xe. Anh ngồi cạnh cửa sổ. Anh nghĩ về con chó, ổ bánh mì và ông già. Chuyến xe muộn cho anh một món quà nhỏ. Món quà đó là thời gian. Anh mỉm cười một chút.

Giờ Tomás có một ý nghĩ lạ. Có lẽ xe buýt đến muộn không phải lúc nào cũng là điều xấu. Khi anh chờ đợi, anh thấy nhiều hơn. Khi anh vội vàng, anh chẳng thấy gì. Anh không muốn xe buýt muộn mỗi ngày. Nhưng hôm nay, khoảng thời gian chậm rãi không tệ đến thế.

questions.explanation: The text says "Tomás waits for the bus in Lima."

questions.prompt: Why is Tomás bored at first?
options: The bus is late and there is no new message
questions.explanation: "The bus is late" and "There is no new message."

questions.prompt: What does the small dog do?
What does the woman near the corner do?
questions.explanation: "A woman sells bread near the corner."

questions.prompt: How do the birds feel about the old man?
options: They are not afraid of him
questions.explanation: "The birds are not afraid of him."
questions.hint: Look at the old man and the birds.

questions.prompt: Why has Tomás never seen these things before?
options: He is new in the city
He is always busy and on his phone
questions.explanation: "He is always busy. He is always on his phone."

questions.prompt: How long does Tomás wait for the bus?
What is the 'small gift' in the story?
questions.hint: Look at the end of the fourth paragraph.

questions.explanation: "He is always busy" means he always has a lot to do.
questions.hint: Think about a person with no free time.

questions.prompt: What is the strange idea Tomás has?
options: A late bus is not always a bad thing
He should never take the bus
questions.explanation: "Maybe a late bus is not always a bad thing."

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the story?
options: Slow waiting time can help you see and enjoy more
questions.explanation: "When he waits, he sees more. When he is fast, he sees nothing."
questions.hint: Think about the whole story, not one detail.

questions.prompt: Does Tomás want the bus to be late every day?
options: No, but today the slow time is not so bad
He does not care at all
questions.explanation: "He does not want the bus to be late every day. But today, the slow time is not so bad."
questions.hint: Look at the very last lines.

blanks.explanation: The bus comes after fifteen minutes, so it is late.
With no message and a late bus, he is bored.
The dog sleeps in the sun.
A woman sells bread near the corner.
An old man feeds the birds.
He is always busy, so he never looks around.
Because the bus is late, he has time.
The bus comes after fifteen minutes.
The late bus gives him a small gift.
When he waits, he sees more.
The slow time is not so bad.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The story says "When he is fast, he sees nothing." Think of a time you were in a hurry. What small thing near you did you not notice?
